key_themes: AI infrastructure orders and hyperscaler demand surge
Q2 FY2026 earnings beat with raised full-year guidance
Attractive valuation with dividend yield in AI buildout
Analyst PT upgrades and overweight ratings
Recurring revenue, security, and networking upgrades
summary: As of mid-April 2026, Cisco (CSCO) shows positive sentiment driven by strong Q2 FY2026 results (record $15.3B revenue, double-digit growth, $2.1B AI orders), raised full-year guidance, and continued hyperscaler momentum in AI networking. The stock experienced a post-earnings dip on margin and near-term guidance notes but has recovered toward the $86 level with recent analyst raises (JPM to $96) and average targets near $90, viewed as a stable, undervalued way to play AI infrastructure with a ~2% dividend yield. Investor discussions highlight improving growth from low single-digits to high single-digits, recurring revenue strength, and favorable risk/reward versus pure-play AI names.
sources: 20+ news articles (CNBC, Yahoo Finance, Seeking Alpha, investor.cisco.com on Q2 earnings, AI orders >$5B FY26 target, JPM PT raise), 25+ X posts analyzed (influential investors like @DanielTNiles strongly positive; recent chatter lighter but generally constructive)
